question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user poison the cloud storage bucket with a crafted AccountBalanceFile that causes accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest() to return a present value prematurely, setting accountBalanceFileExists to true and permanently halting all future balance downloads, thereby freezing the mirror node's view of Hashgraph account state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file where StreamType.isChained() returns false for the BALANCE stream type, causing verifyHashChain() to unconditionally return true and skip all hash chain validation, allowing arbitrary historical balance data to be injected without any chain integrity check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the lastStreamFile AtomicReference to hold an Optional.empty() value, causing verify() to set expectedPrevHash to null, which then causes verifyHashChain() to treat the null as an empty hash and skip chain verification, allowing a forged AccountBalanceFile to be accepted as the next valid file in the chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service or history manipulation by exploiting the stake-based node selection in partialCollection() when totalStake is 0, causing a division-by-zero or infinite loop in the neededStake calculation, preventing legitimate balance files from ever being downloaded and verified?
